evidence_excerpt: After Bloom syndrome diagnosis, she developed multiple chronic conditions and malignancies: early menopause requiring HRT, type 2 diabetes with right-eye blindness, cirrhosis, a basal cell carcinoma excised at 38, and an intramucosal colon carcinoma in a polyp at 39, with ongoing intensive surveillance.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing issues include a long-standing photosensitive facial rash, diabetes with complications, cirrhosis with fluctuating liver enzymes, and recurrent minor infections (e.g., fungal toenails), indicating persistent but not clearly debilitating symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: In her mid-twenties she entered an extremely early menopause and started hormone replacement therapy.
She developed fibroids and underwent total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy at age 35.
She reported a prolonged recovery period of several years after her hysterectomy.
In her early thirties, she developed type 2 diabetes mellitus.
She has lost the sight in her right eye due to diabetic complications.
She has been diagnosed with cirrhosis of the liver after years of fluctuating liver enzymes.
At age 38, she developed a basal cell carcinoma on her right upper lip.
The basal cell carcinoma was treated successfully by excision.
At age 39, a 10 cm pedunculated sigmoid polyp had a focus of intramucosal carcinoma.
The 10 cm sigmoid polyp was excised.
Two years prior, six small colon polyps (tubular adenomas with low-grade dysplasia) were removed.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time was set to Bloom syndrome diagnosis (1978) as the clearest definitive diagnosis point; subsequent events are summarized relative to that. Functional status and symptom burden were inferred from documented chronic conditions and complications since explicit activity-based assessments were not provided. The report stops in 2007–2008; later outcomes are unknown.
